title: Scope Open PR observation failures
goal: Make Open-PR guard observation failures resource-scoped so one broken repository observation blocks only overlapping writes instead of pausing unrelated repositories.

metadata.source_review: Follow-up from PR #177 open-PR claim guard review.
metadata.safety_boundary: Fail closed for the affected resource; do not turn observation failures into implicit allow decisions.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #226: Open-PR observation failures now synthesize fail-closed reservations for the affected git-repository resource only, while non-overlapping repo-write tasks remain eligible and claimable.
validation: Bureau PR #226 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check src/bureau/v2.py tests/test_claim_guard.py -> passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/test_claim_guard.py tests/test_v2.py -q -> passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest -> 355 passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check . -> passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json doctor -> healthy true.
Local before merge: git diff --check -> passed.
Post-merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.

verified_acceptance.resource-scoped-failure: src/bureau/v2.py now converts missing path, repository resolution and gh observation errors into OpenPullRequestReservation blockers for the affected git-repository resource.
verified_acceptance.multi-repo-test: tests/test_claim_guard.py covers a two-repository registry where one repository observation fails and a non-overlapping repo-write task remains eligible and claimable.
verified_acceptance.diagnostic: frontier reasons include open PR guard failure, the affected resource id and the observation diagnostic without exposing secrets.

acceptance.assertion: An observation failure carries enough repository/resource context to synthesize blockers only for overlapping claims.
Tests cover two git repositories where one observation fails and a non-overlapping repo-write task remains eligible.
frontier and explain-next identify the affected repository/resource and failure class without exposing secrets.
